APPLICATION PROMPT

Application Name:
“wassan-vikarabad”

🎯 Objective

Build a full-stack web application using:

Frontend: React JS

Backend: Google Apps Script (Web App API)

Database: Google Spreadsheets

Authentication: Custom login using spreadsheet user table

The application is for field staff work logging, attendance tracking, and reporting.

🧩 MODULES & FEATURES
1️⃣ Home Page (Public View)
UI Components

Hero Image Slider

Auto-scrolling banner images

Images related to field activities

Admin can update images via Google Drive links

Field Gallery Section

Grid gallery layout

Each image tagged with:

Activity Name

Date

Village

Filters:

Activity Name dropdown

Village filter (optional)

Header Menu

Home

Gallery

Sign In

2️⃣ Authentication System
Sign-In Page

Fields:

Username

Password

Validation:

Check credentials from Users sheet

Role-based access:

Field Staff

Project Staff

Admin

After login:

Show dashboard options based on role

3️⃣ Field Staff Dashboard

Menu Options:

Log Works

Calendar Attendance

Workdone Report

Logout

4️⃣ Log Works Module
Form Fields

Date Picker

Visited Village Name (Text)

Activity Name (Text)

Work Details (Textarea)

Status Selection (Top of Form)

Radio buttons:

Working

Leave

Holiday

Conditional Logic

If Leave or Holiday selected:

Show “Reason” textbox (mandatory)

Submit Action

On submit:

Store data in “Work_Log” sheet with columns:

| Date | Username | Village | Activity | Work Details | Status | Reason | Timestamp |

Use Google Apps Script Web App POST API.

5️⃣ Calendar Attendance View
Features

Monthly calendar view

Data fetched from spreadsheet

Color Codes

🟢 Green → Working

🔴 Red → Leave / Holiday

⚪ Blank → No entry

Interaction

Clicking a date:

Opens Log Work form

Auto-fills selected date

6️⃣ Workdone Report Module
Filters

Month dropdown

Year dropdown

Reporting Period Logic

Report period:

26th of Previous Month → 25th of Selected Month

Example:

If user selects March 2026
Report shows: Feb 26 → Mar 25

Report Display

Table columns:

Date

Village

Activity

Work Details

Status

PDF Download

Features:

Generate PDF report

Include:

Staff Name

Reporting Period

Total Working Days

Leave Count

Holiday Count

Download button

PDF generated via Apps Script / HTML → PDF export.

7️⃣ Admin Dashboard

(Admin-only access)

Menu:

Attendance Monitor

User Management

Reports Overview

8️⃣ Admin – Attendance Monitor
Filters

User Selection Dropdown

Month & Year selector

Display

Calendar View

Same color coding as staff

Attendance Summary

Total Working Days

Leaves

Holidays

Missing Entries

Detailed Table

| Date | Village | Activity | Status | Reason |

9️⃣ Admin – User Management
Add User Form

Fields:

Username

Password

Staff Category (Dropdown)

Options:

Field Staff

Project Staff

Admin

Storage

Save in “Users” sheet

Columns:

| Username | Password | Role | Created Date |

🔟 Google Spreadsheet Structure

Create one spreadsheet with tabs:

Sheet 1 — Users

| Username | Password | Role | Created Date |

Sheet 2 — Work_Log

| Date | Username | Village | Activity | Work Details | Status | Reason | Timestamp |

Sheet 3 — Gallery (Optional)

| Image URL | Activity | Village | Date |

🔌 Apps Script Backend APIs

Create Web App APIs:

1. Login API

Validate username & password

2. Submit Work Log API

Insert row in Work_Log

3. Get Attendance API

Fetch logs by user & month

4. Report API

Filter by date range (26 → 25 logic)

5. Add User API

Insert into Users sheet

🎨 UI/UX Requirements

Responsive design

Mobile-friendly

Clean dashboard layout

Calendar with tooltips

Loading indicators

Success/error alerts

🔐 Security Rules

Role-based route protection

Admin routes restricted

Passwords stored securely (hashed if possible)

🚀 Deployment

React app hosted on Firebase / Vercel

Apps Script deployed as Web App

Spreadsheet as database

✨ Extra Features (AI-Suggested Enhancements)

Add these if possible 👇

Photo upload with work log

GPS location capture

Offline form sync

Email report to admin

Monthly performance chart

Export Excel report

Dark mode UI

**Defining Project Scope**

Okay, I'm currently focused on defining the initial project scope for "wassan-vikarabad". It's going to be a React application with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS for the frontend. Field staff work logging, attendance tracking, and reporting are the core functionalities I'm considering. I'm aiming for a comprehensive solution tailored for the specific needs of the project.


**Outlining API Architecture**

I'm now outlining the backend API architecture. Google Apps Script will serve as the Web App API, with Google Sheets as the database. Authentication will be handled by a custom login system using a Spreadsheet user table. I'm also considering a home page with a slider, field gallery, and public access. Role-based routing is also a consideration. I'm also planning Staff Dashboard elements, AI enhancements, and Workdone reports. I'll simulate the GAS connection with a `apiService` for testing.


**Constructing Frontend Structure**

I'm presently building the React app structure. I've decided on including a `metadata.json` for project information and an `index.html` with a Tailwind CDN. I will ensure a React 18 root in `index.tsx`, along with routing and authentication in `App.tsx`. I'll also define shared types and constants, with a services directory and components for various UI elements. Notably, the Google Apps Script code will be provided. Finally, I will provide the pages as mentioned: Home with a hero slider (using picsum) and filtered gallery.


**Refining API Actions**

I'm now refining the GAS API actions; I'll implement `doPost` and `doGet` methods. The script will handle login, work logging, attendance retrieval, user data access, and user addition. I'm mindful of CORS issues and will use `ContentService`. I've also noted that the Gemini API key needs to be separated from the GAS Web App URL and used a placeholder, `GAS_WEBAPP_URL`. Finally, I'm considering using Gemini to analyze logs and summarize activities for the admin section, aligning with my expertise.

To resolve the "Loading..." screen (white blank screen) issue, we need to address a conflict between the **Vite build system** and the **manual Import Map** in your `index.html`. 

### The Problem
Your `index.html` contains an `importmap` pointing to `esm.sh` while your Vercel logs show that **Vite** is already bundling your application during the deployment process. When both are present:
1.  **Vite** bundles all your dependencies (like React, ReactDOM, etc.) into the final production JS file.
2.  The browser's **Import Map** then tries to load a *second* copy of those same dependencies from a CDN at runtime.
3.  This creates a "Multiple copies of React" conflict, which crashes the application immediately before it can render anything, leaving you stuck on the CSS-based loading message.

### The Fix
1.  **Remove the `importmap`**: Vite handles dependency resolution and bundling during the build phase. Removing this avoids runtime conflicts.
2.  **Disable the Service Worker**: Temporarily disabling the complex caching logic in `sw.js` ensures that the browser doesn't keep serving a stale, broken version of the site.
3.  **Harden the Gemini Service**: Added extra safety checks when accessing the `API_KEY` to ensure the app doesn't crash if the environment variable isn't immediately available.

entries.description: Removed the conflicting importmap and simplified index.html. In a Vite-managed environment, having an importmap causes "Multiple copies of React" errors and module resolution conflicts at runtime, which prevents the React application from mounting and results in a permanent "Loading..." screen.
Disabled the Service Worker to prevent caching of stale or broken builds during the troubleshooting phase.
Hardened the Gemini service initialization to handle cases where 'process' might not be fully available in the global scope during early script execution.
